customDomain.title: Custom domain
customDomain.hint: Use a domain you already own for your dynamic QR codes instead of qrcode.numerik360.com. Point it to us first (CNAME), then request it here.
customDomain.pendingHint: Request received — the team activates it manually once the CNAME is verified.
customDomain.failedHint: Activation failed. Contact support for details.

errors.domainTaken: This domain is already in use.

explainer.title: How it works, with a concrete example
explainer.prerequisite: You must already own a domain name, purchased from a registrar like OVH, Gandi, or Namecheap (around $10-15/year). QRHub does not sell domain names — this feature only connects a domain you already own to your QR codes.
explainer.exampleIntro: Your shop is called "My Shop" and you already own {domain}.

step1.title: At your domain provider (not here)
step1.description: Create a CNAME record pointing to us:
step1.result: This creates the subdomain {domain}, pointing to QRHub.
step1.whyGo: Why "go"? It's just a suggestion, not a requirement — a short, common name for this kind of link. You can use anything instead: qr, links, l… or even your main domain directly.

step2.title: Come back here and submit your request
step2.description: Enter {domain} in the field below and click "Save". The status changes to "Pending".

step3.title: Verification (24-48h)
step3.description: Our team checks that the CNAME is correctly configured, then activates your domain. The status changes to "Active".

step4.title: Done, automatically
step4.description: All your dynamic QR codes (existing and future) now show {domain} instead of qrcode.numerik360.com — nothing to reprint.

explainer.pricingNote: How much it costs: this feature is included in your current subscription, no extra payment on QRHub. The domain name itself ({domain}) is a separate purchase from your domain registrar — you likely already have it if you've reached this step.
